skill.name: developing-genkit-js
skill.description: Develop AI-powered applications using Genkit in Node.js/TypeScript. Use when the user asks about Genkit, AI agents, flows, or tools in JavaScript/TypeScript, or when encountering Genkit errors, validation issues, type errors, or API problems.
skill.summary: Develop AI-powered applications using Genkit in Node.js/TypeScript
skill.author: genkit-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 194 potential security issues, primarily CLI command examples in markdown documentation. All flagged patterns are documentation showing Genkit CLI usage (genkit start, npm install, genkit docs:search, etc.). No actual executable code patterns or malicious intent found. The skill provides standard Genkit development patterns for AI application development.

medium_findings.title: CLI Command Examples in Documentation
medium_findings.description: 176 markdown code blocks show CLI commands (npm install, genkit start, etc.). These are documentation examples, not executable code. No user input injection vectors exist.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static analyzer detected CLI commands in markdown but cannot distinguish documentation from executable code. All 176 detections are within markdown code blocks showing legitimate Genkit CLI usage patterns.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Localhost URL
low_findings.description: Documentation references http://localhost:4000 for local development server. Standard development pattern, not a security risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Localhost URL is standard for development documentation. No external network calls or sensitive operations.

low_findings.title: Example URL in Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation uses https://example.com/photo.jpg as placeholder image URL. Standard example domain, not sensitive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: example.com is a reserved domain for documentation and examples. No security concern.

low_findings.title: API Key Reference in Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation mentions GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable. Reminds users to configure their own keys, does not contain or transmit secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation instructs users to set their own environment variables. No actual secret values present in the skill.

content.value_statement: Genkit simplifies AI application development in JavaScript and TypeScript, but navigating its API changes and best practices can be time-consuming. This skill provides expert guidance on flows, tools, and providers to help you build production-ready AI applications quickly.
actual_capabilities: Initialize and configure Genkit projects with provider plugins (Google AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama)
Create AI flows with structured input and output schemas using Zod
Define and register AI tools for multi-step agent workflows
Troubleshoot common Genkit errors including v1.x migration issues
Implement streaming responses and multimodal generation
Set up the Genkit Developer UI for testing and debugging
limitations: Does not include provider API authentication code beyond configuration examples
Does not execute code in sandboxed environments; assumes safe local execution
Does not cover backend deployment or production scaling patterns
Examples focus on Google AI provider; other providers require additional research

use_cases.title: New Project Setup
use_cases.description: Initialize a new Genkit project with proper structure, configure a provider, and create a simple working flow. Ideal for developers getting started with AI application development.

use_cases.title: Error Resolution
use_cases.description: Debug Genkit validation errors, type mismatches, and deprecated API usage. The skill includes a comprehensive common errors reference with solutions.
use_cases.target_user: Developers encountering Genkit runtime or type errors

use_cases.title: Advanced Flow Design
use_cases.description: Build complex multi-step flows with custom tools, streaming responses, and multimodal capabilities (text, image, audio generation).
use_cases.target_user: Experienced AI developers building production applications

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Genkit flow that takes a topic as input and returns a summary. Use the Google AI provider with gemini-2.5-flash model.

prompt_templates.title: Build Multi-Tool Agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Genkit flow with two tools: one to search the web and another to format results. Chain them together in a single flow.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Streaming
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert a regular Genkit flow to use streaming output so users see responses as they are generated.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Genkit Error
prompt_templates.prompt: I am getting a 'configureGenkit is not a function' error when running my Genkit code. The code worked yesterday but I updated some packages. Help me fix this.

output_examples.input: Create a Genkit flow that translates text between languages
output_examples.output: Created flow 'translateText' with input schema (text, sourceLang, targetLang) and output schema (translatedText). Uses googleAI model 'gemini-2.5-flash'. Flow is registered and ready for testing with `genkit flow:run translateText`.

output_examples.input: Help me fix this error: 'response.text is not a function'
output_examples.output: This error indicates you are using pre-1.0 Genkit syntax. In Genkit v1.x, response properties are accessed directly, not as methods. Change `response.text()` to `response.text` in your code. See references/common-errors.md for additional migration notes.

output_examples.input: Set up Genkit in my existing Next.js project
output_examples.output: Detected Next.js project structure. Installing @genkit-ai/next and @genkit-ai/google-genai packages. Creating src/ai directory with genkit.ts configuration. Adding genkit:ui script to package.json. Genkit is now configured and ready for use.

best_practices: Always use the latest model versions (gemini-2.5-flash or newer) and consult the Genkit CLI for current recommendations
Use Zod schemas with .describe() for all input and output fields to guide the LLM behavior
Run type checks (npx tsc --noEmit) after writing Genkit code to catch schema mismatches early
anti_patterns: Do not use deprecated model versions (gemini-1.5-*, gemini-2.0-*) which are decommissioned and will fail
Do not import from internal packages (@genkit-ai/ai, @genkit-ai/core, @genkit-ai/flow) - import from the main 'genkit' package
Do not rely on internal knowledge for error fixes - always consult references/common-errors.md first before other sources

faq.question: Which AI providers does this skill support?
faq.answer: Genkit supports multiple providers including Google AI (default), OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, and Vertex AI. Search docs with 'genkit docs:search plugins' to find specific packages.

faq.question: How do I update the Genkit CLI?
faq.answer: Run 'npm install -g genkit-cli@^1.29.0' to get the latest version. Verify with 'genkit --version'.

faq.question: Why am I getting type errors with my Genkit code?
faq.answer: Most type errors come from deprecated imports. Ensure you import { z, genkit } from 'genkit' and plugins from their specific packages like '@genkit-ai/google-genai'.

faq.question: How do I test my flows locally?
faq.answer: Run 'genkit start -- <your command>' to start the Developer UI. Use 'genkit flow:run <flowName>' for CLI-based testing.

faq.question: Can I use Claude or OpenAI models with Genkit?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the @genkit-ai/oai-compat plugin for OpenAI models or search for Anthropic-specific plugins using 'genkit docs:search anthropic'.

faq.question: What is the difference between flows and tools?
faq.answer: Flows are multi-step AI operations that can call multiple tools and return complex results. Tools are single-purpose functions that can be called by flows or agents.
